mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates directly increase the discount rate for equity valuations, compressing forward price-to-earnings multiples and generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC exceeds 0.05 over a 3-year rolling window or forward returns show positive correlation with rate hikes.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid monthly increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.3 percentage points signals an abrupt labor market deterioration that triggers immediate risk-off positioning and negative equity returns within one week.
failure_condition: Forward 5-day returns remain positive in > 55% of triggered instances over a 5-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real GDP and aggregate retail sales confirms broad-based consumer demand strength, supporting corporate revenue growth and driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Interaction term loses statistical significance (p > 0.10) in quarterly return regressions or joint positive growth yields negative forward returns.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production during a monetary tightening cycle reflects resilient supply-side capacity that offsets higher borrowing costs, generating positive equity returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Signal direction reverses to negative during tightening periods or Rank IC falls below -0.02 in regime-filtered samples.

mechanism_narrative: Monthly CPI inflation exceeding 2.8% triggers immediate sell-offs as markets price in imminent aggressive rate hikes, producing negative S&P 500 returns within one week.
failure_condition: Forward 5-day returns are positive in > 60% of threshold-crossing events over a 4-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects expanding economic activity and rising corporate payrolls, which supports earnings growth and generates positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window or payroll growth shows negative correlation with quarterly returns.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between housing starts and retail sales indicates shifting consumer spending patterns that create sector rotation volatility, resulting in negative aggregate S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Interaction effect becomes statistically insignificant or predicts positive returns when divergence widens.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production during a post-shock recovery phase signals lingering supply chain bottlenecks that compress profit margins, driving negative equity returns within one week.
failure_condition: Signal loses predictive power (Rank IC < 0.02) during recovery regimes or forward returns turn positive despite production declines.
